paper_title: Estimation and Quantitative Genetic Models in Brain Size Studies

methods.description: This method involves estimating brain size using various measurements such as endocranial volume, brain weight, and cranial module. These measurements are used to understand brain size in archaeological specimens and contemporary human populations.

steps.description: Endocranial volume is directly measured by mustard seed, shot or water displacement of endocasts, or estimated from tomographic (CT) or magnetic resonance (MRI) methods.
steps.searchable_summary: measure endocranial volume using physical or imaging methods

steps.description: Brain weight is directly measured from cadavers or estimated from CT or MRI based on brain volume and estimated tissue density.
steps.searchable_summary: measure or estimate brain weight using cadaver data or imaging

steps.description: Cranial module is calculated as the arithmetic mean of three external measurements of the skull: maximum length, maximum breadth, and cranial height.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate cranial module from skull measurements

steps.description: Subtract twice the vault thickness from the external module to consider endocranial volume.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust cranial module for vault thickness

methods.description: This method uses a quantitative genetic model to evaluate the relationship between brain and body size changes, testing the null hypothesis that brain size changes are due to genetic correlation with body size.

steps.description: Determine the amount of change in body size required to explain the observed change in brain size.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate required body size change for observed brain size change

steps.description: Evaluate whether the amount of change in body size is credible given the skeletal record.
steps.searchable_summary: validate body size change against skeletal record

steps.description: Use Lande's equation to model the change in population mean of brain size with a correlated change in body size.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Lande's equation for brain and body size correlation

steps.description: Test the null hypothesis that brain size change is due to genetic correlation with body size.
steps.searchable_summary: test null hypothesis for brain size change correlation
